label: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 21

extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIC Primoz won the ITT by 14 seconds over CORT NIELSEN Magnus, securing his third consecutive Vuelta title by 4:42 over MAS Enric.
tactical_patterns: YATES Adam gained 29 seconds early in ITT before catastrophic second-half collapse, revealing unsustainable pacing strategy on rolling courses.
ROGLIC Primoz nearly went wrong way at critical junction but still beat CORT NIELSEN by 14s, suggesting course reconnaissance weakness.
HAGUE Jack successfully defended podium against YATES ITT attack despite inferior time trialling pedigree, validating 4-minute GC buffer calculation.

rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: admitted second half was toughest he remembered after losing podium, suggesting poor ITT pacing discipline on rolling terrain
rider_signals.CORT NIELSEN Magnus: positive: three stage wins then nearly won Stage 21 ITT, revealing exceptional post-effort recovery for multi-stage winners
rider_signals.HAGUE Jack: positive: held third overall against specialist time triallist YATES despite GC pressure, showing improved ITT resilience
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: neutral: retired during stage on own terms, making ceremonial final appearance
